title: Repair reviewed wait discipline and prove live native behavior
action: Repair only the independently reviewed delta. Change every writable doctrine/procedure surface to say that at least one material task-local action satisfies the progress interval; it does not require exhausting all local work. If completion arrives during the interval, handle its result exactly once before commentary, recompute the relevant live-agent set, and never call wait_agent when that set is empty. Preserve the one-list_agents fallback only when no material work remains, all D1 ownership protections, no authority/urgency exception, and the native-versus-external boundary. Replace whole-file scattered-keyword census logic with a localized normalized contract assertion plus order-sensitive and mutation cases that fail on wait-before-commentary, urgency/no-chatter exceptions, timeout-as-failure, interrupt/redispatch/ownership release, stale completion, zero-agent re-wait, and loss of external carve-out. Add one frozen material-work-remains scenario and clarify scenario 3's exhaustion precondition. Record an isolated root-AGENTS-only replay separately from the combined future-sync replay. Capture two controlled native collaboration traces without inventing output: (1) an ordinary read-only I/O gather over one declared markdown fact; (2) a two-child read-only review wave over distinct doctrine lenses. For each flow, use a short initial bounded wait against deliberately nontrivial child work so at least one real timeout/no-completion is observed; after it, perform at least one genuine task-local implementation/evidence action, handle any completion exactly once, recompute the relevant live set, send concise commentary naming live state and next action, and only then wait again if live agents remain; stop without waiting at zero. Never interrupt, redispatch, release ownership, or poll files to manufacture the trace. Keep .agents read-only and do not touch worktree-isolation. Use the literal judge exactly as stored. Read the exact configured full-repository command from .bee/config.json commands.verify; after changes, run the literal judge, git diff --check, and that full verify as three separate invocations, capturing each exit/output and never inferring conjunction success. Rescue scope: rendered plugin skill trees are generated outputs of the canonical changes and the release manifest is their required hash ledger. Run node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write, inspect that the manifest diff is limited to declared generated/plugin skill entries, then rerun the stored judge, git diff --check, release_manifest --check, and the exact configured full verify separately before cap.

truths: at least one material action satisfies the interval and does not require exhausting all local work
completion arriving during the interval is handled exactly once before commentary
relevant liveness is recomputed and zero live agents forbids another wait
localized order-sensitive and mutation assertions pin D1-D6 on every writable surface
root-only replay proves current Codex deployment independently of canonical procedure text
real native traces cover ordinary delegation and swarm/review flows
the stored literal judge actually exits zero with captured output
generated plugin projections and their release-manifest hashes are synchronized from the canonical feature changes
artifacts: ordinary-gather and swarm/review native trace evidence
updated doctrine spec and worker report
generated Claude/Codex plugin skill projections and updated release manifest
prohibitions: no rewrite of historical cell-2 trace
no .agents writes or false sync claim
no inferred pass from separately green constituents

trace.outcome: Repaired D6/D7 native Codex wait discipline, pinned it with mutation-resistant tests, proved root-only and live native behavior, and synchronized generated plugin projections plus release hashes.
trace.friction: out-of-scope but important: full verification exposed generated plugin and release-ledger coupling; the orchestrator approved same-cell rescue scope.
